title: Tragacantha hendersonii
taxonomy: Tragacantha hendersonii is classified as a species of shrub in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Hedysareae. The genus Tragacantha is a part of the flowering plant family Fabaceae. Further classification within the genus and species is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and to the domain Eukarya. This plant is native to the Hawaiian Islands.

intro: Tragacantha hendersonii, also known as Astragalus hendersonii, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing immune function. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha hendersonii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tragacantha hendersonii is often used to make teas, decoctions, and capsules, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
